subclassFeature.name: Mastermind
entries: Your focus is on people and on the influence and secrets they have. Many spies, courtiers, and schemers follow this subclass, leading lives of intrigue. Words are your weapons as often as knives or poison, and secrets and favours are some of your favourite treasures.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Intrigue
entries: When you choose this subclass at tier 1, you gain proficiency with the Deception, Finesse, and History skills. You also learn two languages of your choice.

Additionally, you can unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute, enabling you to pass yourself off as a native speaker of a particular land, provided that you know the language.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Tactics
entries: Starting at tier 1, you can use the Help action as a bonus action. Additionally, when you use the action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target of that attack can be within 30 feet of you, rather than adjacent to you, provided the target can sense you.

subclassFeature.name: Mastermind Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Mastermind Rogues have access to the following major effect options:

items: You immediately make a Perception check with DC equal to 8 + the target's CR. On a success, the target is Exposed as per the Ranger's {@classFeature Expose Weakness|Ranger|sns|1|sns} feature for 1 round. If the triggering attack killed the target, you may choose another creature within 60 feet of you to be the target of this effect instead, ignoring line of sight.

If you share a language with the target, the target is {@condition charmed|sns} for 1 round. Otherwise, they are {@condition frightened|sns} for 1 round instead. If the triggering attack killed the target, you may choose another creature within 60 feet of you to be the target of this effect instead, ignoring line of sight.

subclassFeature.name: Silver Tongue
entries: Starting at tier 2, you develop your conversational skills during battle to better deceive others. As an action, you can attempt to deceive a creature within 30 feet of you that can sense you. Make a Deception check contested by the target's Insight check. If you succeed, for 1 round or until you use this feature on a different creature, your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks from the target and any attacks against it aided by your Help action have their success tier increased by 1. If you fail the contest, the target becomes immune to this effect for 1 hour.

subclassFeature.name: Soul of Deceit
entries: Starting at tier 3, your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means, unless you allow it. You can present false thoughts by succeeding on a Deception check contested by the mind reader's Insight check.
Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates you are being truthful if you so choose, and you can't be compelled to tell the truth by magic.

subclassFeature.name: Insightful Manipulator
entries: Starting at tier 4, if you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics of your choice:
At the DM's discretion, you might also realise you know a piece of the creature's history or one of its personality traits, if it has any.

subclassFeature.name: Stifling Distraction
entries: Beginning at tier 5, your tactical assistance in battle improves defence as well as offence. Whenever a creature makes an attack aided by your Help action, the attack's target has disadvantage with their own attacks against the attacker for 1 round.
